changes_summary: Revised the plan around the four critique gaps: a single shared `extract_external_urls` helper for stable external indexes, a two-tier source-domain/provenance download safety model, explicit attachment/embed/external kind branches everywhere refs are consumed, and a concrete structured publisher send-unit model that preserves suppressed output, counts, sent IDs, and fallback semantics.
questions: Should YouTube/Vimeo/Twitch links be downloadable for TopicEditor, or should they remain fallback-link-only despite appearing in the curator safelist?
Should production cache storage remain local disk plus DB metadata, or should downloaded files also be copied to durable object storage after this implementation?

success_criteria.criterion: Existing attachment and embed media refs normalize, validate, render, count, and publish exactly as before for current tests.
`normalize_media_ref` accepts `kind: "external"` refs while preserving existing shorthand attachment behavior and rejecting invalid kinds.

One shared deterministic `extract_external_urls` helper is used by source payloads, `get_message_context`, validation, `_resolve_media_url_from_metadata`, rendering fallback lookup, and publish-time resolver lookup.
Source message payloads and `get_message_context` expose compact `media_refs_available` entries for safelisted external URLs, with Discord attachment/embed refs preserved before external refs.
Every downstream media-ref kind branch is explicit for `attachment`, `embed`, and `external`; no non-attachment branch implicitly treats refs as embeds.

External URL extraction and resolution enforce source-domain safelist, metadata-first yt-dlp, timeout, max-size, content-type, Discord upload compatibility, and sanitized logging behavior in unit-tested paths.
yt-dlp is invoked in metadata-only mode before any download attempt, and metadata/download failures fall back without raising into TopicEditor publishing.
Resolved external media is cached by stable URL key plus content hash so repeated publish attempts can reuse cache without re-downloading.

Structured publishing uses explicit send units for text, fallback URLs, and files while preserving suppressed-mode `publish_units`, `flat_messages`, `media_indices`, `media_count`, `flat_message_count`, sent-id accounting, and partial/failure status semantics.

On resolver, download, compatibility, or Discord file-send failure, the publisher sends the original external link in the correct interleaved block position and continues publishing remaining text/units.
_summarize_source_media_counts preserves current attachment/embed count semantics and does not silently inflate existing counts with external fallback URLs.
No code path reintroduces direct LiveTopCreations-style auto-posting at the reaction threshold; reaction-qualified media remains an auto-shortlist-to-watching flow.
Logs and trace metadata describe resolved/downloaded/skipped/fallback outcomes without emitting query tokens or secrets from URLs.
New resolver code keeps pure parsing/normalization helpers separate from subprocess, network, filesystem, and DB side effects.
Tests avoid real network calls, real yt-dlp execution, and real Discord sends by using stubs/fakes.
A manual production smoke test can publish a structured topic containing a Reddit or X external media ref and observe either an attached file or fallback link in the expected block position.

assumptions: External media resolution is lazy and publish-time only for external refs selected in structured block media_refs.
Discord attachments and embeds remain preferred and keep existing attachment/embed media_ref behavior.
The shared `extract_external_urls` helper is the only source of truth for external URL ordering and indexing.
Resolved yt-dlp CDN URLs are allowed only with safelisted source provenance plus content-type, byte-cap, timeout, and Discord upload compatibility checks.
The default byte cap should align with practical Discord bot upload limits and remain configurable with a hard maximum.
Local cache path plus DB metadata is acceptable for implementation; production durability is handled as deployment follow-up.
Structured block publishing is the only publish path that needs external media binding; legacy simple-topic publishing remains unchanged.
Tests use fakes/stubs for subprocess, HTTP, cache, and Discord sends.
